title: Poke Polish Delight
description: A light, indulgent Polish dish featuring poached halibut, floral notes, and a subtle shattering effect from crystallized sugar.
instructions: Line a 9x13 inch baking dish with parchment paper.
In a medium saucepan, combine the sugar, water, and cornstarch.
Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture comes to a boil.
Remove from heat and stir in the floral water.
Add the halibut fillets to the saucepan and coat with the sugar mixture.
Transfer the halibut to the prepared baking dish and poach in the oven for 10-12 minutes or until cooked through.
Meanwhile, prepare the sauce by whisking together the dry sherry, lemon juice, chopped fresh dill, salt, and black pepper.
Once the halibut is cooked, remove it from the oven and brush with the sauce.
Serve the halibut in a mason jar, garnished with additional fresh dill if desired.
To create the shattering effect, sprinkle the crystallized sugar over the top of the halibut just before serving.
